pre_game.message: Hey, I’m H. This is all a bit surreal, isn't it? I’m just trying to keep my head on straight before things kick off—how are you feeling about everything?
Nice to meet you, H. I'm D. "Surreal" is an understatement; I'm feeling pretty on edge because I really need this to go my way. How are you holding up?
I’m hanging in there, though the nerves are definitely kicking in. I’ve got a massive loan hanging over my head, so I’m pretty motivated to make this happen. What's your story?
I get the pressure. I actually lost twenty grand in the stock market just yesterday, so I’m here to fix that mistake and bring something back for my family.
That’s a heavy hit to take right before coming here; I'm aiming for about the same amount to clear my debt, so I guess we both have a lot riding on this.
Exactly. It’s more than just a game when you’re fighting to get back to zero. Hopefully, we can both walk away with what we need.
I really hope so. Getting back to zero would be such a massive weight off my shoulders, and I’m sure your family would feel the same relief.
They definitely would. It’s hard to think about anything else right now. Do you have any idea how this game is actually going to work?
Honestly, I’m as much in the dark as you are, but I’m just ready to do whatever it takes to get that prize pool moving our way.
I’m right there with you. When you’ve got nothing left to lose, you do what you have to. I'm going to see who else is in here, catch you later.
I hear you. Let's just hope there's enough for both of us to get what we need. Good luck out there.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you and B ended up with nothing. I took only my fair share of $1,562 to try to ensure there was enough for everyone, but J, A, and E took far more than their portions.
It's disappointing to hear that J, A, and E took so much, leaving B and me with nothing. I appreciate you trying to stick to a fair share, though it's a shame the group couldn't coordinate better.
